result.markdown:
--- title: "Session 4: Therapeutic Decision Tree — DES vs BMS" subtitle: "Drug-eluting stents vs bare metal stents for coronary artery disease in India" format: html: toc: true code-fold: show code-tools: true --- ## The Clinical Question Coronary artery disease (CAD) is a leading cause of mortality in India. Percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) with stent placement is a standard treatment, and the choice between **drug-eluting stents (DES)** and **bare metal stents (BMS)** has major cost and outcome implications. In 2017, India's NPPA capped stent prices — BMS at approximately ₹7,260 and DES at ₹29,600. As of 2025, revised ceilings stand at approximately ₹10,693 for BMS and ₹38,933 for DES. This dramatically changed the economics of stent choice in India. **The HTA question:** At current Indian price ceilings, is DES cost-effective compared to BMS over a 5-year time horizon? This is a **therapeutic decision tree** — comparing two treatment strategies with different costs and probabilities of downstream events. ## The Decision Problem We compare two strategies for a patient undergoing PCI for stable CAD: 1. **DES (Drug-Eluting Stent):** Higher initial cost, lower restenosis and MACE rates 2. **BMS (Bare Metal Stent):** Lower initial cost, higher restenosis and repeat revascularization rates The decision tree branches on: procedural success → restenosis (requiring repeat revascularization) → major adverse cardiac events (MACE: MI or cardiac death). ## Model Parameters ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # ============================================================ # MODEL PARAMETERS — DES vs BMS Decision Tree # ============================================================ # --- Cohort --- n_cohort <- 1000 # 1,000 patients undergoing PCI # --- Stent Costs (₹) --- # Source: NPPA revised ceiling prices 2025 cost_des_stent <- 38933 # DES ceiling price cost_bms_stent <- 10693 # BMS ceiling price # --- Procedure Costs (₹, excluding stent) --- # Source: Estimated from Indian hospital data, PMJAY/CGHS package rates # Includes catheterisation lab, consumables, hospital stay, medications cost_procedure <- 120000 # Base PCI procedure cost (same for both) # --- Post-PCI Medication Costs (annual, ₹) --- # DES requires longer dual antiplatelet therapy (DAPT): 12 months # BMS requires shorter DAPT: 1-3 months, then single antiplatelet # Source: Indian cardiology practice guidelines cost_dapt_des_annual <- 8000 # Clopidogrel + Aspirin for 12 months cost_dapt_bms_annual <- 3000 # Shorter DAPT + single antiplatelet cost_followup_annual <- 5000 # Annual follow-up visits, tests # --- Procedural Success --- # Source: Indian PCI registry data p_success_des <- 0.97 p_success_bms <- 0.96 # --- Restenosis (within 1 year, requiring repeat revascularization) --- # Source: PMC comparative study (Indian data); international meta-analyses # BMS: 15-20% restenosis requiring intervention # DES: 5-10% restenosis p_restenosis_des <- 0.08 p_restenosis_bms <- 0.20 # --- Cost of Repeat Revascularization (₹) --- # Repeat PCI or CABG # Source: PMJAY package rates cost_repeat_pci <- 180000 # Repeat PCI (more complex than index) cost_cabg <- 250000 # CABG if PCI not suitable # Proportion requiring CABG vs repeat PCI among restenosis cases p_cabg_if_restenosis <- 0.20 # 20% need CABG, 80% get repeat PCI # --- MACE at 5 years (MI, cardiac death) --- # Among patients WITHOUT restenosis # Source: Circulation meta-analysis; 5-year follow-up data p_mace_des_no_restenosis <- 0.08 # 5-year MACE rate, DES, no restenosis p_mace_bms_no_restenosis <- 0.15 # 5-year MACE rate, BMS, no restenosis # Among patients WITH restenosis (after repeat revascularization) p_mace_des_restenosis <- 0.15 p_mace_bms_restenosis <- 0.25 # --- MACE Outcomes --- # Among those with MACE: proportion MI vs cardiac death p_mi_given_mace <- 0.70 # 70% MI p_cardiac_death_given_mace <- 0.30 # 30% cardiac death # --- Costs of MACE Events (₹) --- cost_mi_management <- 200000 # Acute MI management cost_cardiac_death <- 50000 # Terminal care costs # --- Utility Weights (5-year horizon) --- # Source: Adapted from international literature (Sullivan et al.); # No dedicated Indian EQ-5D data for post-PCI; disclaimer applies utility_well_post_pci <- 0.85 # Stable post-PCI utility_restenosis_recovered <- 0.78 # After repeat revascularization utility_post_mi <- 0.65 # Post-MI, surviving utility_death <- 0.00 # Death # --- Discount Rate --- discount_rate <- 0.03 # 3% annual, applied to years 2-5 # --- Time Horizon --- time_horizon <- 5 # years ``` ::: ::: {.callout-note} ## Note on Indian Utility Data Indian-specific EQ-5D utility values for post-PCI patients are limited. The utility weights used here are adapted from international literature. For a formal HTA submission to an Indian HTA body, a local utility study or regionally validated values would strengthen the analysis. ::: ## Building the Decision Tree ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(DiagrammeR) grViz(" digraph des_bms { graph [rankdir=LR, bgcolor='transparent', fontname='Helvetica', nodesep=0.4] node [fontname='Helvetica', fontsize=10] D [label='PCI Stent\nChoice', shape=square, style=filled, fillcolor='#4e79a7', fontcolor='white', width=1.2] DES [label='DES\n₹38,933', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#4e79a7', fontcolor='white'] BMS [label='BMS\n₹10,693', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#e15759', fontcolor='white'] # DES branch DS [label='Success\n(97%)', shape=circle, style=filled, fillcolor='#d4e6f1', fontsize=9] DF [label='Failure\n(3%)', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#f5b7b1', fontsize=9] DR [label='Restenosis\n(8%)', shape=circle, style=filled, fillcolor='#fdebd0', fontsize=9] DNR [label='No Restenosis\n(92%)', shape=circle, style=filled, fillcolor='#d5f5e3', fontsize=9] DM1 [label='MACE\n(15%)', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#f5b7b1', fontsize=9] DW1 [label='Well', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#d5f5e3', fontsize=9] DM2 [label='MACE\n(8%)', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#f5b7b1', fontsize=9] DW2 [label='Well', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#d5f5e3', fontsize=9] # BMS branch BS [label='Success\n(96%)', shape=circle, style=filled, fillcolor='#d4e6f1', fontsize=9] BF [label='Failure\n(4%)', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#f5b7b1', fontsize=9] BR [label='Restenosis\n(20%)', shape=circle, style=filled, fillcolor='#fdebd0', fontsize=9] BNR [label='No Restenosis\n(80%)', shape=circle, style=filled, fillcolor='#d5f5e3', fontsize=9] BM1 [label='MACE\n(25%)', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#f5b7b1', fontsize=9] BW1 [label='Well', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#d5f5e3', fontsize=9] BM2 [label='MACE\n(15%)', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#f5b7b1', fontsize=9] BW2 [label='Well', shape=box, style='filled,rounded', fillcolor='#d5f5e3', fontsize=9] D -> DES D -> BMS DES -> DS DES -> DF DS -> DR DS -> DNR DR -> DM1 DR -> DW1 DNR -> DM2 DNR -> DW2 BMS -> BS BMS -> BF BS -> BR BS -> BNR BR -> BM1 BR -> BW1 BNR -> BM2 BNR -> BW2 } ") ``` ::: {#fig-des-bms-tree .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` Decision tree structure: DES vs BMS for coronary intervention ::: ::: ### Step 1: Initial PCI and Stent Costs ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # --- Total Initial PCI Cost per Patient --- initial_cost_des <- cost_procedure + cost_des_stent initial_cost_bms <- cost_procedure + cost_bms_stent cat("Initial PCI cost per patient:\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Initial PCI cost per patient: ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" DES: ₹", format(initial_cost_des, big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` DES: ₹ 158,933 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" BMS: ₹", format(initial_cost_bms, big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` BMS: ₹ 130,693 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" Difference: ₹", format(initial_cost_des - initial_cost_bms, big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Difference: ₹ 28,240 ``` ::: ::: ### Step 2: Trace Through the Decision Tree ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # ============================================================ # DES ARM # ============================================================ # Procedural outcomes des_success <- n_cohort * p_success_des des_failure <- n_cohort * (1 - p_success_des) # Proceed to CABG # Among successful DES des_restenosis <- des_success * p_restenosis_des des_no_restenosis <- des_success * (1 - p_restenosis_des) # MACE outcomes des_mace_from_restenosis <- des_restenosis * p_mace_des_restenosis des_no_mace_from_restenosis <- des_restenosis * (1 - p_mace_des_restenosis) des_mace_no_restenosis <- des_no_restenosis * p_mace_des_no_restenosis des_no_mace_no_restenosis <- des_no_restenosis * (1 - p_mace_des_no_restenosis) # MACE breakdown (MI vs death) des_total_mace <- des_mace_from_restenosis + des_mace_no_restenosis des_mi <- des_total_mace * p_mi_given_mace des_cardiac_death <- des_total_mace * p_cardiac_death_given_mace cat("=== DES Arm: Patient Flow ===\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` === DES Arm: Patient Flow === ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Successful PCI:", des_success, "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Successful PCI: 970 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Procedural failure (→ CABG):", des_failure, "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Procedural failure (→ CABG): 30 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Restenosis requiring intervention:", round(des_restenosis, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Restenosis requiring intervention: 77.6 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Total MACE at 5 years:", round(des_total_mace, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Total MACE at 5 years: 83 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" - MI:", round(des_mi, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` - MI: 58.1 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" - Cardiac death:", round(des_cardiac_death, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` - Cardiac death: 24.9 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} # ============================================================ # BMS ARM # ============================================================ bms_success <- n_cohort * p_success_bms bms_failure <- n_cohort * (1 - p_success_bms) bms_restenosis <- bms_success * p_restenosis_bms bms_no_restenosis <- bms_success * (1 - p_restenosis_bms) bms_mace_from_restenosis <- bms_restenosis * p_mace_bms_restenosis bms_no_mace_from_restenosis <- bms_restenosis * (1 - p_mace_bms_restenosis) bms_mace_no_restenosis <- bms_no_restenosis * p_mace_bms_no_restenosis bms_no_mace_no_restenosis <- bms_no_restenosis * (1 - p_mace_bms_no_restenosis) bms_total_mace <- bms_mace_from_restenosis + bms_mace_no_restenosis bms_mi <- bms_total_mace * p_mi_given_mace bms_cardiac_death <- bms_total_mace * p_cardiac_death_given_mace cat("\n=== BMS Arm: Patient Flow ===\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` === BMS Arm: Patient Flow === ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Successful PCI:", bms_success, "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Successful PCI: 960 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Procedural failure (→ CABG):", bms_failure, "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Procedural failure (→ CABG): 40 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Restenosis requiring intervention:", round(bms_restenosis, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Restenosis requiring intervention: 192 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Total MACE at 5 years:", round(bms_total_mace, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Total MACE at 5 years: 163.2 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" - MI:", round(bms_mi, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` - MI: 114.2 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" - Cardiac death:", round(bms_cardiac_death, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` - Cardiac death: 49 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} flow_data <- data.frame( Outcome = rep(c("Successful PCI\n(no complications)", "Restenosis\n(repeat intervention)", "MI", "Cardiac Death"), 2), Stent = rep(c("DES", "BMS"), each = 4), Count = c( round(des_no_mace_no_restenosis), round(des_restenosis), round(des_mi), round(des_cardiac_death), round(bms_no_mace_no_restenosis), round(bms_restenosis), round(bms_mi), round(bms_cardiac_death) ) ) flow_data$Outcome <- factor(flow_data$Outcome, levels = c("Successful PCI\n(no complications)", "Restenosis\n(repeat intervention)", "MI", "Cardiac Death")) ggplot(flow_data, aes(x = Outcome, y = Count, fill = Stent)) + geom_col(position = "dodge") + geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("DES" = "#4e79a7", "BMS" = "#e15759")) + labs(x = "", y = "Number of Patients (per 1,000)", title = "Patient Outcomes at 5 Years: DES vs BMS") + theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "bottom") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Patient flow comparison: DES vs BMS per 1,000 patients](des-bms-decision-tree_files/figure-html/fig-patient-flow-1.png){#fig-patient-flow width=672} ::: ::: ### Step 3: Calculate Total Costs ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # --- DES: Total Costs --- # Initial PCI cost_initial_des <- des_success * initial_cost_des + des_failure * cost_cabg # Annual medication and follow-up (5 years, discounted) discount_factor <- sum(1 / (1 + discount_rate)^(0:(time_horizon - 1))) cost_annual_des <- n_cohort * (cost_dapt_des_annual + cost_followup_annual) # Year 1 DAPT is higher; subsequent years are follow-up only cost_medication_des <- n_cohort * cost_dapt_des_annual + # Year 1 n_cohort * cost_followup_annual * discount_factor # All years # Restenosis management cost_restenosis_des <- des_restenosis * ( (1 - p_cabg_if_restenosis) * cost_repeat_pci + p_cabg_if_restenosis * cost_cabg ) # MACE management cost_mace_des <- des_mi * cost_mi_management + des_cardiac_death * cost_cardiac_death # Total total_cost_des <- cost_initial_des + cost_medication_des + cost_restenosis_des + cost_mace_des # --- BMS: Total Costs --- cost_initial_bms <- bms_success * initial_cost_bms + bms_failure * cost_cabg cost_medication_bms <- n_cohort * cost_dapt_bms_annual + n_cohort * cost_followup_annual * discount_factor cost_restenosis_bms <- bms_restenosis * ( (1 - p_cabg_if_restenosis) * cost_repeat_pci + p_cabg_if_restenosis * cost_cabg ) cost_mace_bms <- bms_mi * cost_mi_management + bms_cardiac_death * cost_cardiac_death total_cost_bms <- cost_initial_bms + cost_medication_bms + cost_restenosis_bms + cost_mace_bms cat("=== Cost Summary (cohort of", n_cohort, "patients) ===\n\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` === Cost Summary (cohort of 1000 patients) === ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("DES:\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` DES: ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" Initial PCI: ₹", format(round(cost_initial_des), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Initial PCI: ₹ 161,665,010 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" Medications: ₹", format(round(cost_medication_des), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Medications: ₹ 31,585,492 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" Restenosis mgmt: ₹", format(round(cost_restenosis_des), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Restenosis mgmt: ₹ 15,054,400 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" MACE mgmt: ₹", format(round(cost_mace_des), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` MACE mgmt: ₹ 12,869,960 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" TOTAL: ₹", format(round(total_cost_des), big.mark = ","), "\n\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` TOTAL: ₹ 221,174,862 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("BMS:\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` BMS: ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" Initial PCI: ₹", format(round(cost_initial_bms), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Initial PCI: ₹ 135,465,280 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" Medications: ₹", format(round(cost_medication_bms), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Medications: ₹ 26,585,492 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" Restenosis mgmt: ₹", format(round(cost_restenosis_bms), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Restenosis mgmt: ₹ 37,248,000 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" MACE mgmt: ₹", format(round(cost_mace_bms), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` MACE mgmt: ₹ 25,296,000 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat(" TOTAL: ₹", format(round(total_cost_bms), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` TOTAL: ₹ 224,594,772 ``` ::: ::: ### Step 4: Calculate QALYs ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # --- DES: QALYs over 5 years --- # Patients who are well (no restenosis, no MACE) qaly_des_well <- des_no_mace_no_restenosis * utility_well_post_pci * time_horizon # Patients with restenosis but recovered (no subsequent MACE) qaly_des_restenosis_ok <- des_no_mace_from_restenosis * (utility_well_post_pci * 0.5 + # 6 months pre-intervention utility_restenosis_recovered * (time_horizon - 0.5)) # Patients with MI (surviving) qaly_des_mi <- des_mi * (utility_well_post_pci * 2 + # ~2 years before MI utility_post_mi * (time_horizon - 2)) # Patients with cardiac death (~midpoint) qaly_des_death <- des_cardiac_death * utility_well_post_pci * (time_horizon / 2) # Procedural failures (CABG — assumed similar utility trajectory) qaly_des_failure <- des_failure * utility_restenosis_recovered * time_horizon total_qaly_des <- qaly_des_well + qaly_des_restenosis_ok + qaly_des_mi + qaly_des_death + qaly_des_failure # --- BMS: QALYs --- qaly_bms_well <- bms_no_mace_no_restenosis * utility_well_post_pci * time_horizon qaly_bms_restenosis_ok <- bms_no_mace_from_restenosis * (utility_well_post_pci * 0.5 + utility_restenosis_recovered * (time_horizon - 0.5)) qaly_bms_mi <- bms_mi * (utility_well_post_pci * 2 + utility_post_mi * (time_horizon - 2)) qaly_bms_death <- bms_cardiac_death * utility_well_post_pci * (time_horizon / 2) qaly_bms_failure <- bms_failure * utility_restenosis_recovered * time_horizon total_qaly_bms <- qaly_bms_well + qaly_bms_restenosis_ok + qaly_bms_mi + qaly_bms_death + qaly_bms_failure cat("=== QALY Summary ===\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` === QALY Summary === ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("DES total QALYs:", round(total_qaly_des, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` DES total QALYs: 4130.9 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("DES QALYs per patient:", round(total_qaly_des / n_cohort, 3), "\n\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` DES QALYs per patient: 4.131 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("BMS total QALYs:", round(total_qaly_bms, 1), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` BMS total QALYs: 4018.1 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("BMS QALYs per patient:", round(total_qaly_bms / n_cohort, 3), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` BMS QALYs per patient: 4.018 ``` ::: ::: ### Step 5: ICER Calculation ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # --- Incremental Analysis: DES vs BMS --- inc_cost <- total_cost_des - total_cost_bms inc_qaly <- total_qaly_des - total_qaly_bms icer <- inc_cost / inc_qaly cat("=== Incremental Cost-Effectiveness: DES vs BMS ===\n\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` === Incremental Cost-Effectiveness: DES vs BMS === ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Incremental cost: ₹", format(round(inc_cost), big.mark = ","), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Incremental cost: ₹ -3,419,910 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Incremental QALYs:", round(inc_qaly, 2), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Incremental QALYs: 112.86 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("ICER: ₹", format(round(icer), big.mark = ","), "per QALY gained\n\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ICER: ₹ -30,302 per QALY gained ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} # Interpret against WTP thresholds wtp_1gdp <- 170000 # ~1x GDP per capita India (approximate) wtp_3gdp <- 510000 # ~3x GDP per capita if (icer < 0) { cat("→ DES is DOMINANT (less costly and more effective)\n") } else if (icer < wtp_1gdp) { cat("→ DES is HIGHLY cost-effective (ICER < 1x GDP per capita)\n") } else if (icer < wtp_3gdp) { cat("→ DES is cost-effective (ICER < 3x GDP per capita)\n") } else { cat("→ DES is NOT cost-effective at conventional WTP thresholds\n") } ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` → DES is DOMINANT (less costly and more effective) ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # NNT for avoiding adverse events nnt_restenosis <- round(1 / (p_restenosis_bms - p_restenosis_des)) nnt_mace <- round(1 / ((bms_total_mace - des_total_mace) / n_cohort)) nnt_data <- data.frame( Outcome = c("Avoid 1\nRestenosis", "Avoid 1\nMACE Event"), NNT = c(nnt_restenosis, nnt_mace) ) ggplot(nnt_data, aes(x = Outcome, y = NNT, fill = Outcome)) + geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) + geom_text(aes(label = paste0("NNT = ", NNT)), vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#4e79a7", "#e15759")) + labs(x = "", y = "Number Needed to Treat", title = "How Many Patients Need DES to Prevent One Event?", subtitle = "Compared to BMS over 5-year horizon") + theme_minimal() + expand_limits(y = c(0, max(nnt_data$NNT) * 1.2)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Number needed to treat: DES vs BMS](des-bms-decision-tree_files/figure-html/fig-nnt-visual-1.png){#fig-nnt-visual width=672} ::: ::: ## Visualising the Results ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) library(tidyr) cost_breakdown <- data.frame( Category = c("Initial PCI", "Medications (5yr)", "Restenosis Mgmt", "MACE Mgmt"), DES = c(cost_initial_des, cost_medication_des, cost_restenosis_des, cost_mace_des) / n_cohort, BMS = c(cost_initial_bms, cost_medication_bms, cost_restenosis_bms, cost_mace_bms) / n_cohort ) cost_long <- pivot_longer(cost_breakdown, cols = c(DES, BMS), names_to = "Stent", values_to = "Cost") ggplot(cost_long, aes(x = Stent, y = Cost, fill = Category)) + geom_col(position = "stack") + scale_fill_manual(values = c("Initial PCI" = "#4e79a7", "Medications (5yr)" = "#59a14f", "Restenosis Mgmt" = "#f28e2b", "MACE Mgmt" = "#e15759")) + labs( x = "Stent Type", y = "Cost per Patient (₹)", title = "5-Year Cost Breakdown: DES vs BMS", subtitle = "Indian NPPA ceiling prices (2025)" ) + theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "bottom") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Cost breakdown comparison: DES vs BMS](des-bms-decision-tree_files/figure-html/cost-breakdown-plot-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} outcomes <- data.frame( Outcome = rep(c("Restenosis", "MI", "Cardiac Death"), 2), Stent = rep(c("DES", "BMS"), each = 3), Count = c(round(des_restenosis), round(des_mi), round(des_cardiac_death), round(bms_restenosis), round(bms_mi), round(bms_cardiac_death)) ) ggplot(outcomes, aes(x = Outcome, y = Count, fill = Stent)) + geom_col(position = "dodge") + scale_fill_manual(values = c("DES" = "#4e79a7", "BMS" = "#e15759")) + labs( x = "", y = "Number of Patients (per 1,000)", title = "Clinical Outcomes: DES vs BMS at 5 Years" ) + theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "bottom") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Clinical outcomes per 1,000 patients](des-bms-decision-tree_files/figure-html/outcomes-comparison-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Key Insight Notice what this analysis reveals: DES costs more upfront (₹28,240 more per stent), but **saves money downstream** through fewer repeat revascularisations and fewer MACE events. Whether DES ends up cost-effective, cost-saving, or too expensive depends heavily on the restenosis rate differential and the cost of managing complications. This is exactly the kind of analysis where R shines — you can instantly see how changing one parameter (like the stent price, or the restenosis rate) shifts the conclusion. In Excel, this would require careful re-wiring of formulas across multiple sheets. ## Key References - NPPA India. Revised ceiling prices for coronary stents (2025). - Bangalore S et al. (2012). Short- and long-term outcomes with DES and BMS. *Circulation*. - Kaul U et al. (2017). India and the coronary stent market. *Circulation*. - PMC (2010). Comparative study of restenosis rates in BMS and DES — Indian data. - NEJM (2016). Drug-eluting or bare-metal stents for coronary artery disease. - JAHA (2020).
